root.narration: You have the ball and your teammate is open about ten feet away. A defender is standing between you, but off to the side. How do you get the ball to your teammate?

choices.text: Step toward your teammate and throw a firm chest pass with both hands
Lob the ball high over the defender's head
Try a one-handed throw as hard as you can

outcome.headline: Perfect pass!
outcome.explanation: A chest pass is quick, accurate, and easy for your teammate to catch. By stepping toward your target, the ball zipped right past the defender into your teammate's hands.
outcome.whatToRemember: The chest pass is your best friend. Push the ball from your chest with both hands, thumbs pointing down on the follow-through.

keyTerms.definition: A two-handed pass thrown from your chest directly to a teammate's chest. It is the most accurate and common pass in basketball.

outcome.headline: The ball got there, but just barely
outcome.explanation: A lob pass is slow and floats in the air. Your teammate caught it, but the defender almost jumped up and intercepted it. Lob passes give defenders extra time to react.
outcome.whatToRemember: Lob passes should only be used when a teammate is much taller or there is no other option. Quick, direct passes are usually better.

outcome.headline: Turnover!
outcome.explanation: A one-handed throw is hard to control. The ball sailed wide of your teammate and went out of bounds. Now the other team gets the ball.
outcome.whatToRemember: Use two hands for passes whenever possible. Two hands give you much better control and accuracy.
